pullRequest.title: [DOM-55393] UI add pagination support to endpoints table

nodes.body:
It concerns me a bit the way in which pagination is handled here. What happens if the pagination changes while loading is on? is that page going to be missed? Also something important is that when you work with the prev-curr schema you should not dismiss setting the prev to the curr because of an external condition (in this case loading)

I was thinking that you might be also able to get rid of this prev check by calling the refetch when calling on change pagination, but I am not sure how is that going to work with useRemoteData

why does pagination contains the sorter and filter (I saw it adds it below) state? I assume it has everything as mechanism for being able to refetch when any of the sort, filter or page state changes. If that's the case, the state should not be called `pagination` as it is misleading.

Also a note,  between this state object and the arrangement for checking the prev and current state, it makes me think that we are doing all this things just for making it match with the `useRemoteData` use case. If that's the case, is what useRemoteData offers for the use case here (a loading state) worth all this hacks?
Removed `useRemoteData` to handling the fetch operation by myself
I assume this is an api thing, can you leave a comment for the future here? I assume that true == asc, false == desc and undefined  == none

Have you checked this? Afaik this row key should be unique, by default the table extracts the record id. This is an example:
![image](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/assets/142441807/4053a3ba-8452-4ed2-acd9-6dfcd83fe26c)
